correct_answer: The origins of board games can be traced back to ancient times.
Senet is one of the earliest known board games, dating back to around 3100 BC.
Senet was a game of chance and strategy played on a grid of 30 squares.
The Royal Game of Ur was played by the Sumerians around 2600 BC.
The Royal Game of Ur was found in royal tombs, indicating its importance.
